title: A Pattern Language for Product Management in Complex Organizations
title_short: A Pattern Language for Product Management
author: Isaac Hepworth
description: A reference vocabulary for the dynamics that experienced PMs recognize but have never quite pulled into focus.

patterns.title: Alignment Mirage
patterns.summary: The illusion of shared understanding when language scales faster than meaning.

patterns.title: Cultural Dialects
patterns.summary: The emergence of different sub-languages within an organization.

patterns.title: Explanatory Power vs Precision
patterns.summary: The trade-off between clarity and detail in communication.

patterns.title: Surface Consensus
patterns.summary: The illusion of agreement in a group, masking underlying differences.

patterns.title: Conservation of Organizational Cost
patterns.summary: The idea that costs in an organization are not eliminated, but shifted.

patterns.title: Dual Competence
patterns.summary: The need for both product craftsmanship and organizational navigation skills.

patterns.title: Perverse Correlation
patterns.summary: When a metric becomes a target and its meaning inverts.

patterns.title: Reward Loop Asymmetry
patterns.summary: The tendency for short-term, visible rewards to dominate behavior.

patterns.title: Symbolic Friction
patterns.summary: Introducing a small, visible cost to correct a behavior.

patterns.title: Fluency Capture
patterns.summary: When an organization's feedback mechanisms are captured by those whose incentive is to defeat them.

patterns.title: Creating Gravity
patterns.summary: Using a strong vision to create alignment without direct control.

patterns.title: Elastic Leadership
patterns.summary: The balance between a long-term vision and the team's current reality.

patterns.title: Vision Anchoring
patterns.summary: Framing a product within a larger, aspirational narrative.

patterns.title: Algorithm–Product Gap
patterns.summary: The disconnect between a model's technical success and its value to users.

patterns.title: Ontology Leakage
patterns.summary: When a company's internal model of the world is exposed to users.
